---
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    font-family: 'Trebuchet MS'
    scrollable: true
---

<style>
body { font-family: 'Trebuchet MS', sans-serif; }
.reveal h1, .reveal h2, .reveal h3, .reveal h4 { color: #2980b9; }
.reveal { font-family: 'Trebuchet MS', sans-serif; }

/* QCM Styles */
.qcm-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
  gap: 14px;
  margin-top: 24px;
}
.qcm-option {
  background: #f0f4f8;
  border: 2px solid #bdc3c7;
  border-radius: 10px;
  padding: 14px 18px;
  font-size: 0.95em;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: all 0.3s ease;
  text-align: left;
  font-family: 'Trebuchet MS', sans-serif;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
}
.qcm-option:hover { border-color: #2980b9; background: #ebf5fb; }
.qcm-option.correct {
  background: #d5f5e3;
  border-color: #27ae60;
  color: #1e8449;
  font-weight: 700;
}
.qcm-option.wrong {
  background: #fde8e8;
  border-color: #e74c3c;
  color: #c0392b;
}
.qcm-option .badge {
  font-size: 1.2em;
  min-width: 24px;
}
.qcm-label {
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #2980b9;
  margin-right: 4px;
}
.qcm-question {
  font-size: 1.25em;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #2c3e50;
  background: #eaf2fb;
  border-left: 5px solid #2980b9;
  padding: 14px 18px;
  border-radius: 6px;
  margin-bottom: 6px;
}
.q-number {
  font-size: 0.85em;
  color: #7f8c8d;
  margin-bottom: 6px;
}
.reveal pre { font-size: 0.72em; }
</style>

<script>
function qcm(optionEl, isCorrect) {
  const grid = optionEl.closest('.qcm-grid');
  if (grid.dataset.answered) return;
  grid.dataset.answered = "true";
  const opts = grid.querySelectorAll('.qcm-option');
  opts.forEach(o => {
    o.style.cursor = 'default';
    if (o === optionEl) {
      o.classList.add(isCorrect ? 'correct' : 'wrong');
      o.querySelector('.badge').textContent = isCorrect ? '✅' : '❌';
    } else if (o.dataset.correct === "true") {
      o.classList.add('correct');
      o.querySelector('.badge').textContent = '✅';
    }
  });
}
</script>

<div style="text-align:center; margin-top: 40px; line-height: 1.4;">
  <h1 style="font-size: 2.5em; font-weight: 700; color: #2980b9; margin-bottom: 10px;">
    Programming Language II
  </h1>
  <h2 style="font-size: 1.6em; font-weight: 500; color: #555;">
    Review: Data Types, Collections & Methods
  </h2>
  <div style="border-bottom: 4px solid #e74c3c; width: 45%; margin: 18px auto;"></div>
  <h3 style="margin-top: 25px; font-weight: 700; color: #050E3C">
    By: MIN Sothearith
  </h3>
  <h4 style="font-weight: 700; color: #666;">
    Phnom Penh International University
  </h4>
</div>

## Today's Session

**Two Parts — ~3 Hours:**

::: {.callout-note}
## 📚 Part 1 — Review (Slides 1–30)
Data Types · Collections · Operations · Printing & Formatting · List · Tuple · Dictionary · String & List Methods
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## 🎯 Part 2 — Quiz (40 Questions)
Multiple-choice questions on everything above. Click an answer — see instant feedback!
:::

---

# PART 1: Review

---

# Chapter 1: Data Types

## 1.1 — What is a Data Type?

Every value in Python has a **type** that tells Python what kind of data it is and what operations are allowed.

```python
x = 42          # int
y = 3.14        # float
name = "Alice"  # str
flag = True     # bool
nothing = None  # NoneType
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Use `type()` to check the type of any value:
```python
print(type(42))       # <class 'int'>
print(type("hello"))  # <class 'str'>
print(type(True))     # <class 'bool'>
```
:::

## 1.2 — Numeric Types

**int** — whole numbers (no decimal):
```python
age = 25
year = 2025
negative = -10
```

**float** — numbers with a decimal point:
```python
price = 9.99
temperature = 36.6
pi = 3.14159
```

**Type conversion:**
```python
int(3.9)      # → 3  (truncates, does NOT round)
float(5)      # → 5.0
int("42")     # → 42
str(100)      # → "100"
```

## 1.3 — Strings

A **string** is a sequence of characters enclosed in quotes:

```python
s1 = "Hello"
s2 = 'World'
s3 = """Multi
line string"""
```

Key properties:
- **Indexed** — each character has a position starting at `0`
- **Immutable** — you cannot change individual characters after creation
- **Iterable** — you can loop over each character

```python
s = "Python"
print(s[0])    # P
print(s[-1])   # n
print(s[1:4])  # yth
print(len(s))  # 6
```

## 1.4 — Booleans & None

**bool** — only two values: `True` or `False`

```python
is_active = True
is_admin  = False

# These expressions evaluate to bool:
print(10 > 5)       # True
print(10 == 5)      # False
print(type(True))   # <class 'bool'>
```

**None** — represents the absence of a value:

```python
result = None
if result is None:
    print("No result yet.")
```

::: {.callout-note}
`None` is NOT the same as `0`, `""`, or `False` — but all are **falsy**.
:::

---

# Chapter 2: Operations

## 2.1 — Arithmetic Operations

| Operator | Name | Example | Result |
|----------|------|---------|--------|
| `+` | Addition | `7 + 3` | `10` |
| `-` | Subtraction | `7 - 3` | `4` |
| `*` | Multiplication | `7 * 3` | `21` |
| `/` | Division (float) | `7 / 2` | `3.5` |
| `//` | Floor Division | `7 // 2` | `3` |
| `%` | Modulo | `7 % 2` | `1` |
| `**` | Power | `2 ** 8` | `256` |

```python
print(17 // 5)   # 3  (quotient only)
print(17 % 5)    # 2  (remainder only)
print(2 ** 10)   # 1024
```

## 2.2 — Comparison & Logical Operators

**Comparison** — always return `bool`:

```python
==  !=  >  <  >=  <=
```

```python
print(5 == 5)    # True
print(5 != 3)    # True
print(10 >= 10)  # True
```

**Logical:**

```python
and  # Both must be True
or   # At least one must be True
not  # Reverse the bool
```

```python
age = 20
print(age >= 18 and age < 65)   # True
print(age < 10 or age > 18)     # True
print(not True)                  # False
```

## 2.3 — String Operations

```python
# Concatenation
first = "Hello"
second = "World"
print(first + " " + second)    # Hello World

# Repetition
print("Ha" * 3)                # HaHaHa

# Membership
print("ell" in "Hello")        # True
print("xyz" not in "Hello")    # True

# Length
print(len("Python"))           # 6
```

**Slicing:**
```python
s = "Programming"
print(s[0:4])     # Prog
print(s[::2])     # Pormig  (every 2nd char)
print(s[::-1])    # gnimmargorP  (reversed)
```

---

# Chapter 3: Printing & Formatting

## 3.1 — The print() Function

```python
print("Hello")                    # Hello
print("a", "b", "c")             # a b c
print("a", "b", sep="-")         # a-b-c
print("Hello", end="!")           # Hello! (no newline)
print()                           # blank line
```

**Printing variables:**
```python
name = "Sothearith"
score = 95
print("Name:", name)
print("Score:", score)
```

## 3.2 — f-Strings (Formatted String Literals)

The modern, recommended way to format strings in Python 3.6+:

```python
name = "Alice"
score = 87.5

print(f"Student: {name}")
print(f"Score: {score:.2f}")       # 87.50
print(f"Score: {score:.0f}")       # 88
print(f"Value: {10 * 3}")          # Value: 30
```

**Alignment & padding:**
```python
print(f"{'Name':<15} {'Score':>6}")
print(f"{'Alice':<15} {87.5:>6.1f}")
# Name             Score
# Alice             87.5
```

## 3.3 — Other Formatting Methods

**`.format()` method:**
```python
print("Hello, {}!".format("World"))
print("{0} + {1} = {2}".format(3, 4, 7))
```

**`%` operator (older style):**
```python
print("Name: %s, Age: %d" % ("Bob", 30))
print("Pi = %.4f" % 3.14159)
```

**Escape sequences:**
```python
print("Line1\nLine2")   # newline
print("Tab\there")      # tab
print("She said \"Hi\"")  # quotes inside string
```

---

# Chapter 4: Lists

## 4.1 — What is a List?

A **list** is an ordered, mutable (changeable) collection that can hold items of any type:

```python
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
numbers = [10, 20, 30, 40]
mixed  = [1, "hello", True, 3.14]
empty  = []
```

**Indexing & Slicing:**
```python
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "date"]
print(fruits[0])      # apple
print(fruits[-1])     # date
print(fruits[1:3])    # ['banana', 'cherry']
print(fruits[::-1])   # reversed list
```

## 4.2 — List Methods

| Method | What it does | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `.append(x)` | Add `x` to end | `lst.append(5)` |
| `.insert(i, x)` | Insert `x` at index `i` | `lst.insert(0, 1)` |
| `.remove(x)` | Remove first `x` | `lst.remove("a")` |
| `.pop(i)` | Remove & return item at `i` | `lst.pop()` |
| `.sort()` | Sort in place | `lst.sort()` |
| `.reverse()` | Reverse in place | `lst.reverse()` |
| `.index(x)` | Find index of `x` | `lst.index(3)` |
| `.count(x)` | Count occurrences of `x` | `lst.count(2)` |
| `.clear()` | Remove all items | `lst.clear()` |
| `.copy()` | Shallow copy | `lst2 = lst.copy()` |
| `len(lst)` | Number of items | `len(lst)` |

## 4.3 — List Methods in Action

```python
nums = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6]

nums.append(7)
print(nums)            # [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6, 7]

nums.remove(1)         # removes first 1
print(nums)            # [3, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6, 7]

popped = nums.pop()    # removes last item
print(popped)          # 7

nums.sort()
print(nums)            # [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9]

print(nums.count(1))   # 1
print(nums.index(5))   # 4
```

---

# Chapter 5: Tuples

## 5.1 — What is a Tuple?

A **tuple** is an ordered, **immutable** (unchangeable) collection:

```python
point   = (3, 7)
colors  = ("red", "green", "blue")
single  = (42,)       # ← comma required for single item!
empty   = ()
no_paren = 1, 2, 3    # parentheses optional
```

**Why use tuples instead of lists?**

- Data that should NOT change (coordinates, RGB values, dates)
- Slightly faster than lists
- Can be used as dictionary keys (lists cannot)

```python
point = (3, 7)
# point[0] = 10   ← TypeError! Tuples are immutable
print(point[0])   # 3 ✅ reading is fine
```

## 5.2 — Tuple Operations

```python
t = (10, 20, 30, 20, 40)

# Indexing & slicing (same as list)
print(t[1])         # 20
print(t[-1])        # 40
print(t[1:3])       # (20, 30)

# Methods (only 2!)
print(t.count(20))  # 2
print(t.index(30))  # 2

# Length
print(len(t))       # 5

# Unpacking
x, y = (3, 7)
print(x, y)         # 3 7

a, *rest = (1, 2, 3, 4)
print(a, rest)      # 1 [2, 3, 4]
```

---

# Chapter 6: Dictionaries

## 6.1 — What is a Dictionary?

A **dictionary** stores data as **key: value** pairs. Keys are unique and immutable; values can be anything:

```python
student = {
    "name": "Alice",
    "age": 20,
    "gpa": 3.8
}

print(student["name"])    # Alice
print(student["gpa"])     # 3.8
```

**Accessing safely with `.get()`:**
```python
print(student.get("age"))          # 20
print(student.get("score", 0))     # 0 (default if key missing)
```

## 6.2 — Dictionary Methods

| Method | What it does |
|--------|-------------|
| `d[key] = val` | Add or update |
| `del d[key]` | Delete a key |
| `d.get(k, default)` | Safe access |
| `d.keys()` | All keys |
| `d.values()` | All values |
| `d.items()` | All (key, value) pairs |
| `d.update({...})` | Merge another dict |
| `d.pop(key)` | Remove and return value |
| `key in d` | Check membership |
| `len(d)` | Number of pairs |

```python
d = {"a": 1, "b": 2}
d["c"] = 3
print(list(d.keys()))    # ['a', 'b', 'c']
print(list(d.values()))  # [1, 2, 3]
for k, v in d.items():
    print(k, "→", v)
```

---

# Chapter 7: String Methods

## 7.1 — Essential String Methods

```python
s = "  Hello, World!  "

s.strip()           # "Hello, World!"   (remove whitespace)
s.lower()           # "  hello, world!  "
s.upper()           # "  HELLO, WORLD!  "
s.replace("World", "Python")   # "  Hello, Python!  "
s.split(", ")       # ['  Hello', 'World!  ']
", ".join(["a","b","c"])       # "a, b, c"
```

## 7.2 — More String Methods

```python
s = "Hello, Python!"

s.startswith("Hello")   # True
s.endswith("!")         # True
s.find("Python")        # 7  (index where it starts)
s.count("l")            # 2
s.isdigit()             # False
s.isalpha()             # False (has comma & space)
s.capitalize()          # "Hello, python!"
s.title()               # "Hello, Python!"
s.center(20, "*")       # "***Hello, Python!***"
s.zfill(20)             # left-pads with zeros
```

::: {.callout-important}
String methods return **new strings** — they do NOT modify the original (strings are immutable)!
:::

---

## Review Summary

| Topic | Key Points |
|-------|-----------|
| **Data Types** | `int`, `float`, `str`, `bool`, `None` — use `type()` to check |
| **Operations** | `+  -  *  /  //  %  **` — comparison returns `bool` |
| **Printing** | `print()`, f-strings `f"{var:.2f}"`, `sep=`, `end=` |
| **List** | Ordered, mutable, `[]` — `.append()`, `.sort()`, `.pop()` |
| **Tuple** | Ordered, immutable, `()` — `.count()`, `.index()`, unpacking |
| **Dictionary** | Key-value, `{}` — `.keys()`, `.values()`, `.items()`, `.get()` |
| **String Methods** | `.strip()`, `.lower()`, `.split()`, `.join()`, `.replace()`, `.find()` |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Ready for the Quiz?
40 questions ahead — click your answer and see if you're right! 🎯
:::

---

# PART 2: Quiz Time! 🎯

---

## Q1 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 1 of 40 — Data Types</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What is the data type of the value <code>3.14</code> in Python?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> int</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> float</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> str</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> double</button>
</div>

## Q2 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 2 of 40 — Data Types</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does <code>type("123")</code> return?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> &lt;class 'int'&gt;</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> &lt;class 'float'&gt;</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> &lt;class 'str'&gt;</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> &lt;class 'num'&gt;</button>
</div>

## Q3 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 3 of 40 — Data Types</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What is the result of <code>int(7.9)</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> 8</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 7</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 7.9</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Error</button>
</div>

## Q4 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 4 of 40 — Data Types</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Which value is <strong>falsy</strong> in Python?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> "False"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 1</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 0</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> "0"</button>
</div>

## Q5 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 5 of 40 — Data Types</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What is the result of <code>str(True)</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> "1"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> "True"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> True</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Error</button>
</div>

## Q6 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 6 of 40 — Operations</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What is the result of <code>17 % 5</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> 3</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 2</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 3.4</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> 5</button>
</div>

## Q7 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 7 of 40 — Operations</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What is the result of <code>2 ** 10</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> 20</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 100</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 1024</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> 512</button>
</div>

## Q8 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 8 of 40 — Operations</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does <code>7 // 2</code> return?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> 3.5</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 3</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 4</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> 1</button>
</div>

## Q9 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 9 of 40 — Operations</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does <code>"Ha" * 3</code> produce?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> "Ha Ha Ha"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> "HaHaHa"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> Error</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> "Ha3"</button>
</div>

## Q10 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 10 of 40 — Operations</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What is the result of <code>"ell" in "Hello"</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> True</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> False</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 1</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Error</button>
</div>

## Q11 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 11 of 40 — Printing & Formatting</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does <code>print("a", "b", sep="-")</code> output?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> a b</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> a-b</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> "a"-"b"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> ab</button>
</div>

## Q12 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 12 of 40 — Printing & Formatting</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Given <code>x = 3.14159</code>, what does <code>f"{x:.2f}"</code> produce?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> "3.1"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> "3.14"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> "3.141"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> "3.15"</button>
</div>

## Q13 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 13 of 40 — Printing & Formatting</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What character does <code>\n</code> represent in a string?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> Tab</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> Newline</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> Backslash</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Space</button>
</div>

## Q14 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 14 of 40 — Printing & Formatting</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Which prints <code>Hello!</code> without a trailing newline?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> print("Hello!", nl=False)</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> print("Hello!", end="")</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> print("Hello!", sep="")</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> print("Hello!", "\0")</button>
</div>

## Q15 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 15 of 40 — Printing & Formatting</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What is the output of <code>print(f"{2 + 3 * 4}")</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> 20</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 14</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> "2 + 3 * 4"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Error</button>
</div>

## Q16 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 16 of 40 — Lists</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Given <code>lst = [10, 20, 30, 40]</code>, what is <code>lst[-1]</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> 10</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 30</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 40</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Error</button>
</div>

## Q17 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 17 of 40 — List Methods</div>
<div class="qcm-question">After <code>lst = [1,2,3]</code> and <code>lst.append(4)</code>, what is <code>lst</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> [4, 1, 2, 3]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> [1, 2, 3, 4]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> [1, 2, 3]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Error</button>
</div>

## Q18 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 18 of 40 — List Methods</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does <code>lst.pop()</code> do by default?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> Removes the first item</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> Removes and returns the last item</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> Clears the entire list</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Returns the last item without removing</button>
</div>

## Q19 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 19 of 40 — List Methods</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Given <code>lst = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5]</code>, what does <code>lst.count(1)</code> return?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> 1</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 2</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 3</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> True</button>
</div>

## Q20 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 20 of 40 — Lists</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Given <code>lst = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]</code>, what is <code>lst[1:4]</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> [10, 20, 30]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> [20, 30, 40]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> [20, 30, 40, 50]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> [10, 20, 30, 40]</button>
</div>

## Q21 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 21 of 40 — List Methods</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What is the difference between <code>.remove(x)</code> and <code>.pop(i)</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> They are identical</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> .remove() uses index; .pop() uses value</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> .remove() uses value; .pop() uses index and returns the item</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> .pop() deletes all occurrences</button>
</div>

## Q22 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 22 of 40 — Tuples</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Which of the following is a valid single-element tuple?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> (42)</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> (42,)</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> [42]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> {42}</button>
</div>

## Q23 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 23 of 40 — Tuples</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What happens if you try to change a tuple element: <code>t = (1,2,3); t[0] = 9</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> t becomes (9, 2, 3)</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> t becomes [9, 2, 3]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> TypeError — tuples are immutable</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> SyntaxError</button>
</div>

## Q24 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 24 of 40 — Tuples</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does tuple unpacking do in: <code>x, y = (3, 7)</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> x = (3,7) and y = (3,7)</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> x = 3 and y = 7</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> x = 7 and y = 3</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Error</button>
</div>

## Q25 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 25 of 40 — Tuples</div>
<div class="qcm-question">How many methods does a tuple have (built-in)?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> 0</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 2 — count() and index()</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 5</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Same as list</button>
</div>

## Q26 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 26 of 40 — Dictionaries</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Given <code>d = {"a": 1, "b": 2}</code>, what does <code>d.get("c", 0)</code> return?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> KeyError</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> None</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 0</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> "c"</button>
</div>

## Q27 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 27 of 40 — Dictionaries</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Which method returns all key-value pairs as tuples?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> .keys()</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> .values()</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> .items()</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> .pairs()</button>
</div>

## Q28 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 28 of 40 — Dictionaries</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What happens when you assign <code>d["name"] = "Bob"</code> if key <code>"name"</code> already exists?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> Error — duplicate key</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> A second entry is added</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> The existing value is overwritten</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Nothing happens</button>
</div>

## Q29 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 29 of 40 — Dictionaries</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Which of these can be used as a dictionary key?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> A list</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> A tuple</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> A dictionary</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> A set (mutable)</button>
</div>

## Q30 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 30 of 40 — Dictionaries</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Given <code>d = {"x": 10}</code>, what does <code>d.pop("x")</code> return?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> "x"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 10</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> {"x": 10}</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> None</button>
</div>

## Q31 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 31 of 40 — String Methods</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does <code>"  Hello  ".strip()</code> return?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> "Hello  "</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> "  Hello"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> "Hello"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> "hello"</button>
</div>

## Q32 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 32 of 40 — String Methods</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does <code>"a,b,c".split(",")</code> return?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> "a b c"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> ["a", "b", "c"]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> ("a", "b", "c")</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> {"a", "b", "c"}</button>
</div>

## Q33 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 33 of 40 — String Methods</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does <code>"-".join(["x", "y", "z"])</code> return?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> ["x-y-z"]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> "x-y-z"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> "xyz"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Error</button>
</div>

## Q34 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 34 of 40 — String Methods</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does <code>"Hello Python".find("Python")</code> return?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> True</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 1</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 6</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> "Python"</button>
</div>

## Q35 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 35 of 40 — String Methods</div>
<div class="qcm-question">After <code>s = "hello"</code>, does <code>s.upper()</code> change <code>s</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> Yes — s becomes "HELLO"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> No — strings are immutable; it returns a new string</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> It raises an error</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> It modifies s only if assigned back</button>
</div>

## Q36 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 36 of 40 — Collections (Mixed)</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Which collection is <strong>ordered AND mutable</strong>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> List</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> Tuple</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> String</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> int</button>
</div>

## Q37 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 37 of 40 — Collections (Mixed)</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What is the main difference between a list and a tuple?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> Lists use () and tuples use []</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> Lists are mutable; tuples are immutable</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> Lists can only hold numbers</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Tuples are not ordered</button>
</div>

## Q38 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 38 of 40 — Collections (Mixed)</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What is the output of <code>len({"a": 1, "b": 2, "c": 3})</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> 6</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> 3</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> 2</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Error</button>
</div>

## Q39 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 39 of 40 — String Slicing</div>
<div class="qcm-question">Given <code>s = "Python"</code>, what is <code>s[::-1]</code>?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> "Python"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> "Pytho"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> "nohtyP"</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> Error</button>
</div>

## Q40 / 40

<div class="q-number">Question 40 of 40 — Mixed Review</div>
<div class="qcm-question">What does <code>list("abc")</code> produce?</div>
<div class="qcm-grid">
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">A.</span> ("a", "b", "c")</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="true"  onclick="qcm(this, true)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">B.</span> ["a", "b", "c"]</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">C.</span> {"a", "b", "c"}</button>
  <button class="qcm-option" data-correct="false" onclick="qcm(this, false)"><span class="badge">·</span><span class="qcm-label">D.</span> "abc"</button>
</div>

---

## 🎉 Quiz Complete!

<div style="text-align:center; margin-top: 40px;">
  <h2 style="color: #27ae60;">Well done, everyone!</h2>
  <p style="font-size: 1.3em; margin-top: 20px;">You've reviewed all the fundamentals of Programming Language II.</p>
</div>

::: {.callout-tip}
## 🚀 Next Class
We move into **Functions, Modules, and File Handling** — building real, reusable programs!
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## 📌 Practice at Home
Pick any 5 questions you got wrong and write a Python program that demonstrates the correct answer.
:::
